title: microspermum repens
intro: Microspermum repens, also known as the "creeping toothache plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Microspermum repens include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for oral health issues and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
